title.narrative: Inclusive Access to and Sustainable Management of Land and Water Resources

description.narrative: The program will facilitate access to over 30,000 dunoms, will reclaim/rehabilitate 3000 dunoms and provide water resources for over 5000 dunoms of agricultural lands. The program also includes activities to help farmers adapt with climate change and learn new farming technologies such as integrated Soil Management and integrated Pest Management Techniques. The strength of the program formulates from the enthusiasm it spreads in local community for the participation in land development activities as was witnessed by the Midterm review. This spirit of cooperation between local community, civil society and local government provides a nurturing environment for local agricultural development.

Over All Objective: Improve sustainable access and management of land and water resources for food production of Palestinian agricultural producers, including smallholders, and their organizations, and promoting women's land rights. Specific Objective 1: Improve inclusive sustainable agricultural production through land resource management, enhancing Integrated Soil Fertility Management , and creating platform knowledge of climate change adaptation. Specific Objective 2: Improve efficient collective water resource management and increase inclusive access to water resources to enhance the productivity of the agricultural lands. Specific Objective 3: Working with different Stakeholders including the PA, consortium member organizations, and civil society organizations, to promote and lobby for an inclusive and sustainable Agricultural Policy.
Palestinian Farmers (Females and Males), Cooperatives, Water User Associations, and University Students

participating-org.narrative: Economic and Social Development Center Palestine

owner-org.narrative: This identifier refers to the Program "INCLUSIVE ACCESS TO AND SUSTAINABLE MANAGEMENT OF LAND AND WATER RESOURCES"

recipient-country.narrative: 100% of the Budget is implemented in the West Bank

policy-marker.narrative: Improve sustainable access and management of land and water resources for food production of Palestinian agricultural producers, including smallholders, and their organizations, and promoting women's land rights.

description.narrative: Conduct environmental impact assessment study & training for staff on EIA and the training was completed on EIA with work being done to study EIA in Land Reclamation and Water Activities

description.narrative: Conducting environmental awareness and practical training for farmers, University students and volunteers including providing and implementing new methods to adapt climate change effects such as: low tunnels, crop selection practices, planting period shift.

sector.narrative: Conducting environmental awareness and practical training for farmers, University students and volunteers including providing and implementing new methods to adapt climate change effects such as: low tunnels, crop selection practices, planting period shift.

Conduct annual summer camps for youth to raise their awareness on environmental issues
Introduce New Agricultural techniques through 2 Pilots
Potato Trails
Heavy work started for 50 donums which includes completion of Large hammer work for 50 donums
Updated the Database With 6 Reports on 6 Localities

sector.narrative: Update the Database with New Localities

description.narrative: Heavy Work including finishing 243 Donums Heavy Machinery Work, 11,803 m2 Retaining walls, 1,467 m3 Water Harvesting Cisterns

sector.narrative: Land Reclamation Heavy Work

description.narrative: Light Work done for 87 donums of Plowing, 119 donums of Cleaning, 776 Seedlings Planted, 64 donums were planted with seeds, 27.5 donums were fenced

sector.narrative: Land Reclamation

description.narrative: Main Irrigation Pipelines 1.385 km

description.narrative: Circular Steel Balance Water Tank 1000 m3 50% achieved

description.narrative: Circular Steel Balance Water Tank 500 m3 0.5 tank
